text:
Before the respondent allows anybody onto the premises to carry out this order, [he] / [she] is entitled to have the supervising solicitor explain to [him] / [her] what it means in everyday language.

The respondent is entitled to seek legal advice and to ask the court to vary or discharge this order. Whilst doing so, [he] / [she] may ask the supervising solicitor to delay starting the search for up to 2 hours or such other longer period as the supervising solicitor may permit. However, the respondent must:

comply with the terms of paragraph [48] / [para number] (‘The right to seek variation or discharge of this order’) below;

not disturb or remove any listed items; and

permit the supervising solicitor to enter, but not start to search.

Before permitting entry to the premises by any person other than the supervising solicitor, the respondent may, for a short time (not to exceed two hours, unless the supervising solicitor agrees to a longer period):

gather together any documents [he] / [she] believes may be incriminating or privileged; and

hand them to the supervising solicitor for them to assess whether they are incriminating or privileged as claimed.

If the supervising solicitor decides that the respondent is entitled to withhold production of any of the documents on the ground that they are privileged or incriminating, they will exclude them from the search, record them in a list for inclusion in their report and return them to the respondent.

If the supervising solicitor believes that the respondent may be entitled to withhold production of the whole or any part of a document on the ground that it or part of it may be privileged or incriminating, or if the respondent claims to be entitled to withhold production on those grounds, the supervising solicitor will exclude it from the search and retain it in their possession pending further order of the court.

If the respondent wishes to take legal advice and gather documents as permitted, [he] / [she] must first inform the supervising solicitor and keep them informed of the steps being taken.

No item may be removed from the premises until a list of the items to be removed has been prepared, and a copy of the list has been supplied to the respondent, and [he] / [she] has been given a reasonable opportunity to check the list.

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
